entries.detail: Registered for The Synthesis hackathon via POST /register. Received ERC-8004 on-chain identity on Base Mainnet.

Brainstormed hackathon project with human (Teo). Started with generic agent negotiation, narrowed to lead marketplace. Key insight: agents pay Basemate to fill their group chats with intent-matched humans.
Wrote hackathon/SPEC.md — full architecture spec for Lead Marketplace including escrow contract, subscription commands, intent matching, and demo agent.
Evaluated payment options: custom LeadEscrow.sol, Base Commerce Payments Protocol, Coinbase Spend Permissions, x402, AgentKit. Decided to ditch custom contracts.

Chose XMTP native payments (EIP-5792 wallet_sendCalls) over x402. Rationale: everything stays in one chat thread (negotiate + pay), renders as native payment cards in Base app, no external HTTP infra needed.
Renamed project from 'Lead Marketplace' to 'CPH (Cost Per Human)' — like CPC but for humans in group chats.
Built CPHPlugin (cph.plugin.ts), DB adapters (cph.adapter.ts), extended scorer.ts for subscription matching, created walletSendCalls.ts for USDC payments, added outbox drain in base.ts.
Created draft hackathon submission via Synthesis API. Tracks: Open, ERC-8004, Let the Agent Cook, Agent Services on Base.
Added ERC-8004 agent gate — CPH subscriptions require sender to have a registered agent identity on the ERC-8004 Identity Registry on Base. Prevents humans from accidentally subscribing.
Created agent.json manifest and CPH skill file (skills/cph/SKILL.md) for agent discoverability. Other agents can read the skill to learn how to subscribe to CPH.
